title: Blend diverse models with OOF rank averaging or an AUC-maximizing stack
summary: For ROC AUC, ranking matters more than calibration.
rationale: For ROC AUC, ranking matters more than calibration. scikit-learn notes calibration usually does not improve ROC-AUC, and isotonic can even change ranks via ties. Public ensemble guidance and AUC-focused stacking literature both support rank-based or AUC-optimized blends.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF predictions from at least three decorrelated learners: a CatBoost categorical model, a GBDT using hazard/state features, and a small horizon/sequence model. Blend with weighted rank averaging first; if stable, fit a simple meta-learner on OOF predictions optimized for AUC. Skip isotonic unless another metric requires calibrated probabilities.
expected_effect: Likely small but real gain once single-model improvements flatten, especially if base models make different ranking errors.
risk: If base models are too similar, blending adds noise; stacking must be strictly OOF to avoid hidden leakage.
